channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: California Governor’s Race Remains Too Close to Call
item.description: Steve Hilton, a Republican, and Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, were leading in election night returns. Tom Steyer, another Democrat, urged patience as more votes were being counted.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: It’s the first time since California began using the top-two primary system that a governor’s race has been so close.

item.title: Democratic Voters Acted Strategically in a Turbulent California Election
item.description: Without a dominant candidate in the governor’s race, Democratic voters ultimately wanted to ensure that their party wasn’t shut out of the general election.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Minh Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kevin Stull, right, fills out a ballot as barber Bryan Diaz waits to give a haircut at Ana’s Hair Salon on Election Day in the Mission District of San Francisco.

item.title: Republican support for same-sex marriage has fallen by 18 percentage points since 2022.
item.description: summary
item.dc:creator: Ruth Igielnik
item.category: Same-Sex Marriage, Civil Unions and Domestic Partnerships
item.media:credit: Maansi Srivastava/The New York Times
item.media:description: New York City Pride March in 2023.

item.title: Marni von Wilpert and Jim Desmond Advance in a San Diego-Area House Race
item.description: Ms. von Wilpert’s win in Tuesday’s open primary eliminated Democrats’ fears that two Republicans would advance to November in the 48th Congressional District.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

item.title: Incumbent Karen Bass Advances to L.A. Mayoral Election in November
item.description: Her opponent remains undetermined.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler
item.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass at the Los Angeles County Democratic Watch Party on Tuesday.

item.title: Wiener and Chan Will Face Off in House Race to Succeed Nancy Pelosi
item.description: State Sen. Scott Wiener and Supervisor Connie Chan were the top two finishers in the open primary to represent San Francisco as Nancy Pelosi retires.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight
item.media:credit: Anastasiia Sapon for The New York Times
item.media:description: State Sen. Scott Wiener at a campaign event during his run for San Francisco’s main congressional seat.

item.title: A Trump Endorsement Falls Flat: 4 Election Takeaways From Iowa and Beyond
item.description: The president’s pick for governor of Iowa lost his primary, while Democrats in the state chose their nominee in what they hope will be a competitive Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Scott Olson/Getty Images
item.media:description: Representative Randy Feenstra, a Republican, lost his primary bid for governor of Iowa despite a late endorsement from President Trump.

item.title: Zach Lahn Wins Republican Primary for Iowa Governor
item.description: Zach Lahn’s victory was a rare case this year in which a candidate defeated an opponent backed by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman and Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: KC McGinnis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Zach Lahn at an event in Clive, Iowa, in May.

item.title: Representative Brad Sherman to Face Larry Thompson in November
item.description: The 15th-term congressman will face a Republican film producer after they both advanced in a nonpartisan House primary in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

item.title: Gregg Hull Wins G.O.P. Nomination to Be New Mexico Governor
item.description: Gregg Hull, the former mayor of Rio Rancho, a suburb of Albuquerque, will try to break the Democrats’ hold on the southwestern state.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:credit: Morgan Lee/Associated Press
item.media:description: Gregg Hull was one of three Republican candidates seeking the nomination for governor of New Mexico.

item.title: Becerra’s Heritage Appeals to Many Latino Voters, but Some Shrug
item.description: If Xavier Becerra wins the primary, he has a strong chance of becoming California’s first Latino governor since 1875. That’s a draw for some Latino voters, but other factors are driving their votes.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Xavier Becerra attended a phone banking event at the SEIU 2015 offices on Saturday.

item.title: Pacific Palisades Voters Remain Frustrated by Fire Aftermath
item.description: Residents of the Pacific Palisades neighborhood of Los Angeles headed to the polls on Tuesday. Many are waiting for their homes to be rebuilt.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters in Pacific Palisades cast their ballots almost 18 months after much of their community was scorched by a wildfire.

item.title: U.S. Is Said to Be Investigating George Santos Over Kalshi Betting
item.description: The former congressman was referred to federal authorities after he bet on his own attendance at the State of the Union address, a person familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany and Sharon LaFraniere
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The inquiry into former Representative George Santos is the third insider trading case to come to light in recent weeks.

item.title: Spencer Pratt’s L.A. Mayor Campaign Is Fueled by Out-of-Town Cash
item.description: Mr. Pratt, a former reality TV star, has proved adept at raising funds among people who cannot vote for him. His rivals in the race have taken a more local approach.
item.dc:creator: Ken Bensinger
item.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Spencer Pratt, a candidate for Los Angeles mayor, campaigning last week.

item.title: These voters feel like L.A. city residents, but their ballots say otherwise.
item.description: After hearing about the Los Angeles mayor’s race for months, some voters realized Tuesday that they don’t have a say in it.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Stock
item.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
item.media:description: In Culver City, one of the cities surrounded by Los Angeles, residents cannot participate in the Los Angeles mayor’s race they’ve been following.

item.title: 9 Counties to Watch in the California Governor’s Race
item.description: The race might not be called on election night, but these counties could provide signals for what lies ahead.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Radio Host With Trump’s Support Wins Republican House Primary in Montana
item.description: Aaron Flint defeated three other Republicans, including Christi Jacobsen, the Montana secretary of state.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: John Stember/Montana Free Press
item.media:description: Aaron Flint is aiming to replace Representative Ryan Zinke, who is not running for re-election.

item.title: Late-Arriving Ballots Could Lean Democratic in California
item.description: Democratic voters have been returning their ballots later than Republicans in this year’s primaries, which will make it difficult to interpret the results on election night.
item.dc:creator: Luke Vrotsos and Christine Zhang
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: In recent years, more than 80 percent of ballots in California have been cast by mail. They’re sorted and processed in large facilities over a period of weeks.

item.title: Sanchez wins Democratic nod for New Mexico land commissioner
item.description: The tightly fought contest to control 9 million acres of state land revolved around which candidate could beat back President Trump and his public land chief, New Mexico’s Steve Pierce.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: Juan Sanchez, a candidate or Commissioner of Public Lands, in downtown Santa Fe, New Mexico.

item.title: Katie Porter: 5 Facts About the Candidate for California Governor
item.description: The former House member built her reputation by grilling corporate executives, and she has a strong command of policy. But questions about her demeanor have been a drag on her campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Katie Porter, a former member of Congress and the only woman among the top candidates for California governor, has demonstrated a strong command of policy. But her campaign has struggled to recover from viral videos that showed her berating an aide and a reporter.

item.title: Nithya Raman: 5 Facts About the Candidate for Los Angeles Mayor
item.description: Ms. Raman, a progressive Democrat, surprised many allies when she entered the Los Angeles mayor’s race at the last minute.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Nithya Raman, a progressive member of the Los Angeles City Council, has drawn comparisons to New York’s mayor, Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: Top Texas Election Official to Resign, Months Before General Contest
item.description: Jane Nelson, the Texas secretary of state, was appointed to the role overseeing statewide elections in 2023 by Gov. Greg Abbott.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Adam Powell/USA Today Network, via Reuters
item.media:description: Jane Nelson, the top election official in Texas, did not give a reason for her departure.

item.title: Josh Turek, a Paralympian, Wins the Democratic Senate Primary in Iowa
item.description: Mr. Turek, a state lawmaker, beat Zach Wahls in a contest that tested the party’s anti-establishment fervor.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer
item.media:credit: KC McGinnis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Josh Turek, a self-described “prairie populist,” cast himself as a candidate who could sway independents and Republicans.

item.title: Voters in Los Angeles Say They ‘Don’t Have a Lot of Good Choices’
item.description: Many said they were struggling to choose from candidates they saw as uninspiring in both the mayor’s and governor’s races.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan and Maeve Reston
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters cast their primary ballots on Tuesday at the headquarters building of the Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority.

item.title: Deb Haaland wins nomination in New Mexico governor’s race
item.description: Ms. Haaland, a former Interior secretary, took a big step toward making history as the first Native American woman to be a governor when she won the primary in her Democratic state.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:credit: Nina Riggio for The New York Times
item.media:description: Deb Haaland, former secretary for the Interior Department, spoke to a group gathered at a Veterans Helping Veterans building in Gallup, New Mexico, in April.

item.title: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Republican-Friendly Map in Alabama
item.description: In the first major case since the justices narrowed the Voting Rights Act, the court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map preferred by Republicans that leaves the state with only one majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The Supreme Court ruled in 2023 that an Alabama congressional district map with only one majority-Black district diluted the power of Black voters.

item.title: Ashley Hinson Wins the Republican Senate Primary in Iowa
item.description: Representative Hinson, a third-term lawmaker, was the favorite of Republican leaders and donors. The general election is expected to be unusually competitive.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer
item.media:credit: KC McGinnis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Ashley Hinson promised to be President Trump’s “top ally in the United States Senate.”

item.title: Karen Bass: 5 Facts About the Incumbent Los Angeles Mayor
item.description: The incumbent Los Angeles mayor started her term with strong approval ratings, but she lost support with voters after the fires last year that devastated the region.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Karen Bass sailed into the Los Angeles mayor’s office four years ago, but she faces a much tougher election this time after her handling of last year’s fires in the region disenchanted many residents.

item.title: Perry Wins G.O.P. Nomination for New Mexico Land Commissioner
item.description: Michael Perry says he is pragmatic, but Democrats fear he would advance the agenda of President Trump and the New Mexican nominated to head the Bureau of Land Management, Steve Pearce.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: Michael Perry, a Republican candidate for New Mexico Commissioner of Public Lands, at his home in Roswell, New Mexico.

item.title: How Does California’s ‘Top Two’ Voting System Work?
item.description: California voters can choose any primary candidate, regardless of party, and two candidates from the same party can potentially face off in the general election.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Representative Ashley Hinson, Senate Candidate in Iowa
item.description: Ms. Hinson, a former journalist who is supported by President Trump, won the Republican nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: KC McGinnis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Ashley Hinson speaks at the Iowa Faith & Freedom Coalition’s Spring Kickoff in Clive, Iowa, on May 1.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About the Iowa Senate Candidate Josh Turek
item.description: A gold medalist on the U.S. men’s wheelchair basketball team, Mr. Turek is running as a “common-sense moderate.”
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Scott Olson/Getty Images
item.media:description: Josh Turek represents a conservative-leaning section of Iowa as a state lawmaker.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About the Iowa Senate Candidate Zach Wahls
item.description: The progressive Democrat went viral as a teenager with a speech defending marriage equality.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Hannah Fingerhut/Associated Press
item.media:description: Zach Wahls, a candidate for U.S. Senate, talks with voters in Des Moines, Iowa, on May 23.

item.title: Spencer Pratt: 5 Facts About the Candidate for Los Angeles Mayor
item.description: The reality TV star has used his status as a victim of the Palisades fire to mount an outsider campaign portraying Los Angeles officials as incapable of solving the city’s problems.
item.dc:creator: Conor Dougherty
item.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Spencer Pratt, a reality TV star, has used his status as a victim of last year’s Palisades fire to mount a mayoral campaign against the incumbent, Karen Bass.

item.title: Polls in South Dakota Show Competitive Race in GOP Primary for Governor
item.description: Four candidates are competing in a race that will advance to a runoff if no one tops 35 percent.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Swalwell Departure Scrambled Race for California Governor
item.description: Eric Swalwell was one of the leading contenders, but when he dropped out amid sexual assault allegations, his Democratic rivals saw a chance to win more voters.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times

item.title: Steve Hilton: What to Know About the California Governor Candidate
item.description: Mr. Hilton was the right-hand man of Prime Minister David Cameron in Britain and a Fox News host before he ran for California governor.
item.dc:creator: Danny Hakim
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Steve Hilton, who was a top aide to British Prime Minister David Cameron and then became a Fox News host, has been endorsed by President Trump in the California governor’s race.

item.title: Josh Turek and Zach Wahls Make Final Pitches in Senate Primary
item.description: Josh Turek and Zach Wahls, the Democratic candidates for U.S. Senate, made their final pitches as voters took to the polls on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kruse

item.title: War Games and Warnings on Strait of Hormuz Went Unheeded by Trump
item.description: Over the past two decades, Iran repeatedly threatened to close down the waterway. President Trump underestimated Iran’s ability to do so.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz has become a huge source of leverage in negotiations with President Trump.

item.title: Graham Platner Meets With Senate Democrats Amid Texting Scandal
item.description: Mr. Platner, the likely Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine, met in Washington with several senators. Chuck Schumer, the minority leader, dodged questions about his confidence in Mr. Platner.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/Getty Images
item.media:description: Graham Platner met with Democratic senators in Washington days after reports that he had sent sexual messages to women outside his marriage.

item.title: Pushing Treatment, Trump Administration Limits Housing Aid for Homeless
item.description: Congress and a court blocked a previous effort to revamp homeless programs. Even a scaled-back version marks the sharpest change in homelessness policy in a generation.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeParle
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Scott Turner, the housing secretary, said that the model known as Housing First “failed Americans by warehousing the vulnerable without results.”

item.title: When to Expect Results in the June 2 Primaries
item.description: Voters in six states have primaries today. Election officials predict that results will come in late Tuesday or early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson
item.media:credit: Rachel Mummey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voting in Cumming, Iowa, on Tuesday.

item.title: California House Primaries Offer Clues in Battle for Control of Congress
item.description: Proposition 50 has created a handful of key races where Democrats will choose candidates to pit against vulnerable Republican incumbents. Generational challenges have infused other contests.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters stand in line in Sacramento last November to cast ballots for Proposition 50, the redistricting initiative that passed overwhelmingly and led to a new House map more favorable to Democrats.

item.title: Tom Steyer: 5 Facts About the Candidate for California Governor
item.description: He is a billionaire who appeals to many progressive groups in California.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Heather Knight
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tom Steyer, a billionaire and former hedge fund manager, has campaigned as a progressive outsider who favors raising taxes and expanding government programs.

item.title: How a Republican Switcheroo Stifled Competition to Succeed Steve Daines in Montana
item.description: Senator Steve Daines withdrew his candidacy for re-election moments before the filing deadline, and then endorsed Kurt Alme in what appeared to be a coordinated swap to preclude competition.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times, Thom Bridge/Independent Record, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Senator Steve Daines, left, a Republican, withdrew from his re-election bid just minutes before the filing deadline. The move cleared the way for Kurt Alme, right, to avoid major competition in the Republican Senate primary.

item.title: Billionaires and Silicon Valley Have Flooded California’s Races With Cash
item.description: Tom Steyer has spent a staggering $216 million on his campaign for governor. Tech titans have lined up behind one of his rivals. And in other races, rich donors are spending big.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tom Steyer, a former hedge fund investor and a longtime megadonor to liberal causes, has lavishly self-funded his bid for California governor.

item.title: Roman Catholic Priest Sentenced to Life in Prison for Sexual Assault
item.description: Father Anthony Odiong, who served in Texas and Louisiana, had been found guilty of abusing multiple women. Prosecutors say the abuse almost certainly resulted in the birth of a child.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

item.title: Administration Argues for Power Over Philadelphia Slavery Memorial
item.description: More than 100 onlookers packed a ceremonial courtroom for legal arguments before an appeals court about the presentation of history at George Washington’s former residence.
item.dc:creator: Mattathias Schwartz
item.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
item.media:description: Empty plaques hang where signs about slavery were removed at Independence National Historical Park in Philadelphia earlier this year. Some of the placards and videos are now back in place at the site.

item.title: Thomas Kean Jr., Missing NJ Congressman, Issues Statement Promising ‘Transparency’
item.description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr., a Republican who has been absent from Congress and the campaign trail since March, suggested it could be weeks before he returned to work.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Francisco Seco/Associated Press
item.media:description: Aides to Representative Thomas Kean Jr. have said he is recovering from a “personal medical condition” but have provided no details. Nor did the congressman’s statement on Tuesday.
